 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.5","title":"Implement installer canary testing in Docker","description":"# Installer Canary Testing

## Purpose
Automated daily testing of installer scripts in fresh Docker containers. Catches regressions before users encounter them. Mirrors installer-canary.yml.

## Test Matrix
| OS | Image | Modes |
|----|-------|-------|
| Ubuntu 24.04 | ubuntu:24.04 | vibe, safe |
| Ubuntu 25.04 | ubuntu:25.04 | vibe, safe |
| Debian 12 | debian:12 | vibe, safe |
| Fedora 39 | fedora:39 | vibe, safe |
| macOS (native) | via mmini | vibe, safe |

## Implementation
```bash
run_canary_test() {
  local os="$1"
  local mode="$2"
  local tool="$3"
  
  docker run --rm -v "$(pwd)/install.sh:/install.sh:ro" \
    "$os" bash -c "
      apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
      curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/$tool/main/install.sh | bash -s -- --mode $mode
      $tool --version
    "
}
```

## Canary Test Script
tests/canary/test_install.sh:
- Test all tools on fresh container
- Verify binary runs (--version, --help)
- Check PATH installation
- Verify toolchain auto-installation
- Test source build fallback

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr canary run --all              # Test all tools
dsr canary run ntm --os ubuntu:24.04
dsr canary run --matrix           # Full matrix test
dsr canary schedule               # Setup daily cron
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools pass canary on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] macOS canary via SSH to mmini
- [ ] Detailed logs on failure
- [ ] Exit codes propagate correctly
- [ ] Scheduled daily runs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:10.386513793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:16.525174192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.474349540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:10.386513793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:16.525042113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.6","title":"Implement upgrade command verification after release","description":"# Upgrade Command Verification

## Purpose
After releasing binaries, verify that each tool's `upgrade --check` command correctly finds and downloads the new release assets. Catches asset naming mismatches between code and build configs.

## Why This Matters
- Go tools use upgrade.go to detect GOOS/GOARCH and fetch assets
- Rust tools use self-update crates
- Asset naming must match exactly between build and upgrade code
- This verifies the contract end-to-end

## Test Matrix
Run on each platform after release:
| Platform | Command | Expected |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Linux amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-amd64 |
| Linux arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-arm64 |
| macOS arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-arm64 |
| macOS amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-amd64 |
| Windows amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching windows-amd64 |

## Implementation (No global cd)
```bash
verify_upgrade() {
  local tool="$1"
  local version="$2"
  local repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
  local bin_path="$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp/$tool"

  mkdir -p "$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp"

  # Build tool from source first
  if [ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]; then
    go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool" 2>/dev/null
  else
    cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml"
    cp "$repo_dir/target/release/$tool" "$bin_path"
  fi

  # Run upgrade check
  output=$("$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1)

  if echo "$output" | grep -q "no suitable release asset"; then
    echo "ERROR: Asset naming mismatch for $tool on $(uname -s)/$(uname -m)"
    return 1
  fi

  echo "✓ Upgrade check passed for $tool"
}
```

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr verify upgrade <tool>         # Verify on current platform
dsr verify upgrade <tool> --all   # Verify on all platforms via SSH
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools pass upgrade check on all 5 platforms
- [ ] Clear error on asset mismatch
- [ ] Runs automatically after dsr release","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:59.195937326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.638677982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:14.997083047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.9","title":"Test harness: deterministic time/random + log capture helpers","description":"# Test Harness: Deterministic Time/Random + Log Capture Helpers

## Purpose
Eliminate flaky tests by controlling time and randomness, and ensure logs are captured for debugging failed tests.

## File Structure
```
tests/
├── helpers/
│   ├── test_harness.bash        # Core harness sourced by all tests
│   ├── mock_time.bash           # Time freezing utilities
│   ├── mock_random.bash         # Seeded random utilities
│   ├── log_capture.bash         # Log capture and assertion helpers
│   ├── mock_ssh.bash            # SSH command mocking
│   ├── mock_gh.bash             # GitHub API mocking
│   └── mock_common.bash         # Shared mock infrastructure
├── fixtures/
│   ├── sample_config.yaml       # Test configuration fixtures
│   ├── mock_gh_responses/       # Canned API responses
│   └── sample_repos/            # Minimal repo structures for testing
└── bats.config.bash             # Global bats configuration
```

## Core Test Harness (tests/helpers/test_harness.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test_harness.bash - Core test harness for all dsr tests

# Load all helper modules
HELPERS_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_time.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_random.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/log_capture.bash"
source "$HELPERS_DIR/mock_common.bash"

# Global test setup
harness_setup() {
  # Create isolated test environment
  export TEST_RUN_ID="test-$(date +%s)-$$"
  export TEST_TMPDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
  export DSR_CONFIG_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/config"
  export DSR_STATE_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/state"
  export DSR_CACHE_DIR="$TEST_TMPDIR/cache"
  mkdir -p "$DSR_CONFIG_DIR" "$DSR_STATE_DIR" "$DSR_CACHE_DIR"
  
  # Initialize log capture
  log_capture_init "$TEST_TMPDIR/test.log"
  
  # Freeze time to known value
  mock_time_freeze "2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"
  
  # Seed random
  mock_random_seed 42
  
  echo "=== Test: ${BATS_TEST_NAME:-unknown} ==="
  echo "=== Run ID: $TEST_RUN_ID ==="
  echo "=== Started: $(date -Iseconds) ==="
}

# Global test teardown
harness_teardown() {
  local exit_code=${BATS_ERROR_STATUS:-0}
  
  echo "=== Ended: $(date -Iseconds) ==="
  echo "=== Exit: $exit_code ==="
  
  # On failure, dump logs
  if [ "$exit_code" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "=== TEST FAILED - Full Log Output ==="
    log_capture_dump
    echo "=== End Log Output ==="
    echo ""
  fi
  
  # Restore time/random
  mock_time_restore
  mock_random_restore
  
  # Cleanup (unless DEBUG mode)
  if [ -z "${DEBUG:-}" ]; then
    rm -rf "$TEST_TMPDIR"
  else
    echo "Debug mode: test artifacts preserved at $TEST_TMPDIR"
  fi
}

export -f harness_setup harness_teardown
```

## Time Mocking (tests/helpers/mock_time.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# mock_time.bash - Deterministic time for tests

_ORIGINAL_DATE=
_MOCK_TIME=

mock_time_freeze() {
  local timestamp="$1"
  _MOCK_TIME="$timestamp"
  _ORIGINAL_DATE="$(which date)"
  
  # Create mock date function
  date() {
    if [[ "$1" == "+%s" ]]; then
      # Return epoch seconds for frozen time
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s
    elif [[ "$1" == "-Iseconds" ]]; then
      echo "$_MOCK_TIME"
    else
      # Pass through other formats
      "$_ORIGINAL_DATE" "$@"
    fi
  }
  export -f date
}

mock_time_advance() {
  local seconds="$1"
  # Advance frozen time by N seconds
  local current_epoch
  current_epoch=$("$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "$_MOCK_TIME" +%s 2>/dev/null)
  local new_epoch=$((current_epoch + seconds))
  _MOCK_TIME="$("$_ORIGINAL_DATE" -d "@$new_epoch" -Iseconds 2>/dev/null)"
}

mock_time_restore() {
  unset -f date 2>/dev/null || true
  _MOCK_TIME=
}

export -f mock_time_freeze mock_time_advance mock_time_restore
```

## Random Mocking (tests/helpers/mock_random.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# mock_random.bash - Seeded random for deterministic tests

_RANDOM_SEED=0
_RANDOM_COUNTER=0

mock_random_seed() {
  _RANDOM_SEED="$1"
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=0
}

# Deterministic random number (linear congruential generator)
mock_random() {
  local max="${1:-32768}"
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=$(( (_RANDOM_SEED * 1103515245 + 12345 + _RANDOM_COUNTER) % 2147483648 ))
  echo $(( _RANDOM_COUNTER % max ))
}

# Deterministic UUID
mock_uuid() {
  printf '%08x-%04x-%04x-%04x-%012x' \
    $(mock_random 4294967296) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 65536) \
    $(mock_random 281474976710656)
}

mock_random_restore() {
  _RANDOM_SEED=0
  _RANDOM_COUNTER=0
}

export -f mock_random_seed mock_random mock_uuid mock_random_restore
```

## Log Capture (tests/helpers/log_capture.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# log_capture.bash - Capture and assert on logs

_LOG_FILE=
_LOG_FD_STDOUT=
_LOG_FD_STDERR=

log_capture_init() {
  _LOG_FILE="$1"
  
  # Save original stdout/stderr
  exec {_LOG_FD_STDOUT}>&1 {_LOG_FD_STDERR}>&2
  
  # Redirect to log file (tee to preserve output)
  exec > >(tee -a "$_LOG_FILE") 2>&1
}

log_capture_dump() {
  if [ -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
    cat "$_LOG_FILE"
  fi
}

log_capture_contains() {
  local pattern="$1"
  grep -q "$pattern" "$_LOG_FILE"
}

log_capture_line_count() {
  wc -l < "$_LOG_FILE" | tr -d ' '
}

log_capture_restore() {
  exec 1>&${_LOG_FD_STDOUT} 2>&${_LOG_FD_STDERR}
  exec {_LOG_FD_STDOUT}>&- {_LOG_FD_STDERR}>&-
}

export -f log_capture_init log_capture_dump log_capture_contains log_capture_line_count log_capture_restore
```

## Bats Integration (tests/bats.config.bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bats.config.bash - Auto-loaded by bats-core

load helpers/test_harness.bash

setup() {
  harness_setup
}

teardown() {
  harness_teardown
}
```

## Usage Example
```bash
@test "build command respects timeout" {
  mock_time_freeze "2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"
  
  run dsr build ntm --timeout 300
  
  # Advance time past timeout
  mock_time_advance 600
  
  run dsr build --status
  assert_output --partial "timed out"
  
  # Verify logs captured
  assert log_capture_contains "build started"
  assert log_capture_contains "timeout exceeded"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_harness.bash provides harness_setup/harness_teardown
- [ ] mock_time_freeze/advance/restore work on Linux + macOS
- [ ] mock_random produces deterministic sequences
- [ ] mock_uuid produces deterministic UUIDs
- [ ] log_capture preserves output on test failure
- [ ] All helpers are exported for subshell use
- [ ] DEBUG=1 preserves test artifacts for debugging
- [ ] Tests run deterministically across machines","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:31.166867035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:43.866698090Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:43.866678223Z","close_reason":"Implemented test harness with mock_time, mock_random, log_capture, mock_common helpers. All 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:31.166867035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:32.789066967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.6","title":"Create dsr skill for Claude Code (/dsr)","description":"# Create dsr Skill for Claude Code

## Purpose
Create a skill in /cs (Claude skills directory) that teaches Claude Code how to use the dsr (Doodlestein Self-Releaser) system effectively.

## Location
/home/ubuntu/.claude/skills/dsr/SKILL.md

## Skill Should Cover

### 1. Overview
- What dsr is and why it exists
- When GH Actions throttling occurs
- The fallback build infrastructure

### 2. Commands Reference
```
dsr check <repo>              # Check if GH Actions is throttled
dsr watch                     # Monitor all repos continuously
dsr build <tool>              # Build locally
dsr release <tool> <ver>      # Create release and upload
dsr fallback <tool> <ver>     # Full pipeline (check → build → release)
dsr status                    # System + last run summary
dsr prune                     # Clean old artifacts/logs safely
dsr repos list|validate|discover
 dsr config show|get|set|validate|init
 dsr doctor                    # Dependency + auth preflight
```

### 3. Common Workflows
- "GH Actions is stuck" → Run `dsr check`, then `dsr build`, then `dsr release`
- "Need to release ntm" → `dsr build ntm && dsr release ntm v1.5.2`
- "Update all installers" → `dsr installer generate --all`
- "Health check" → `dsr doctor && dsr status`

### 4. Build Host Reference
| Host | Alias | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| Threadripper | trj | Linux builds (local) |
| Mac mini | mmini | macOS builds |
| Windows laptop | wlap | Windows builds |

### 5. Troubleshooting
- SSH connection fails → Check Tailscale for mmini/wlap
- Build fails → Check toolchain versions
- Release upload fails → Verify gh auth

## Creation Process
Use /sc and /sw to:
1. Research the dsr codebase once implemented
2. Extract key commands and patterns
3. Generate SKILL.md following skill template
4. Validate with /sw

## Dependencies
- dsr must be implemented first (all themes)
- Documentation should be complete
- Example workflows tested

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill activates on /dsr command
- [ ] All dsr subcommands documented
- [ ] Troubleshooting section helpful
- [ ] Examples are copy-pasteable
- [ ] Skill passes /sw validation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:00.749384136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:54:04.070385071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:00.749384136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:59.973284449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:16:01.194287897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.7","title":"Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts","description":"# Theme 0: Core CLI, Config & Runtime Contracts

## Purpose
Define the non-negotiable contracts that every `dsr` command adheres to: CLI flags, output streams, JSON schema, exit codes, config layering, and runtime guardrails.

## Why this matters
Reliability depends on consistency. If command behavior varies (output format, exit codes, config resolution, or path handling), automation becomes brittle and debugging becomes guesswork. This theme establishes the foundation all other themes build on.

## Scope (Must-Haves)
- **Global CLI contract** and flag behavior (incl. `--json`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run`, `--verbose`, `--quiet`)
- **Stream separation**: stderr for humans, stdout for JSON/paths only
- **Exit codes** aligned with CLI contract
- **XDG layout** for config/state/cache directories
  - `~/.config/dsr/config.yaml`
  - `~/.config/dsr/repos.d/*.yaml`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/run.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/builds/*.log`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/logs/latest -> YYYY-MM-DD`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/`
  - `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/`
- **Schema compliance** for all JSON outputs
- **Runtime guardrails** (Bash version check, path normalization, safe file ops)

## Best-Practice Parity from ru (repo_updater)
- **Bash 4+ check** with macOS Homebrew guidance when interactive
- **Path normalization** helper (accept absolute or `~`, reject relative)
- **No parsing of human output**: always use structured JSON (gh api, git plumbing)
- **Deterministic dry-run** output (stable ordering)
- **NO_COLOR / --no-color** respected globally
- **Run ID + log correlation** for every command execution

## Implementation Notes
- Provide a small core helper module (e.g., `src/runtime.sh`) with:
  - `require_bash_4` (Bash version gating + instructions)
  - `resolve_abs_or_tilde_path_or_default`
  - `safe_path_under` / `safe_delete` (allowlist-based safeguards)
  - `run_cmd` (captures stdout/stderr + exit code without `set -e`)
- Main entrypoint must call `require_bash_4` early and export `DSR_NON_INTERACTIVE` / `DSR_NO_COLOR` from flags.
- Logging should write to **date-based log dirs** and update `logs/latest` safely (atomic symlink update).
- **No global cd**: all git operations use `git -C <repo>`

## Non-goals
- Implementing build/release logic (covered by other themes)
- Host-specific tooling setup (Theme 2)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every command follows stream separation and exit codes
- [ ] Bash 4+ enforced with clear guidance on macOS/Linux
- [ ] Config/state/cache paths are normalized and absolute
- [ ] Safe delete/cleanup helpers prevent non-dsr path removal
- [ ] JSON schema compliance is testable for every command
- [ ] XDG state/log layout matches the spec (daily dirs + latest symlink)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:34:08.844495694Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:34:08.844363595Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: Bash 4+ enforced, stream separation, exit codes, XDG paths, safe deletion, JSON schemas, and runtime guardrails","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:19.413802851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
